title: astragalus microcephalus subsp. pycnocladus
intro: Astragalus microcephalus subsp. pycnocladus, also known as Pycnocladus, is a type of flowering plant in the legume family (Fabaceae) that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Pycnocladus include reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and potentially aiding in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and asthma. Pycnocladus is typically prepared as a dietary supplement in the form of capsules, tablets, or teas, and is also used in traditional medicine to make decoctions and infusions.
